title: Direct Talk
subTitle: In June 2025, Kobayashi Yoshitsugu published a trailblazing paper, illuminating the evolutionary process of large tyrannosaurs. He explains how such research helps us consider the future of humanity.

title: IMPACTS:Climates Change the World
subTitle: Experts use scientific data and the latest discoveries to analyse the impact of global warming and explore potential solutions.

title: NHK World Japan
subTitle: A look at the rich and fascinating Edo era of Japan, which was dominated by a deep love for the arts. The traditional woodblock prints called Ukiyo-e blossomed during this period of time.

title: Medical Frontiers
subTitle: Faecal microbiota transplantation transfers gut bacteria from a healthy donor's stool into a patient's colon. It is being explored as a treatment for ulcerative colitis, Parkinson's disease and ADHD.

title: Hit the Road
subTitle: A scenic drive through Iwate's rich nature, starting from Morioka station, heading to the picturesque Jodogahama beach, with a detour to Tono, known for its kappa legends.

title: Through the Kitchen Window
subTitle: In a small fishing town, Wajima Eiko crafts wonderful dishes from the unsold catch at the fish market where she has worked for over 50 years.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Design X Stories
subTitle: Analogue is making a comeback, offering a human appeal to our five senses that digital lacks. Explore designs that reveal the charm and potential of analogue.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Asia Insight
subTitle: An in-depth look at the realities of Asia today, a dynamic region which faces political, economic, cultural, and technological change.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Demining Ukraine : Uncovering an Invisible Enemy
subTitle: As a large portion of Ukraine's territory remains contaminated by landmines, we follow a Japanese scientist's use of radar technology to support demining efforts in the war-torn country.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Chatroom Japan
subTitle: Foreign residents talk about their experience of living in Japan and what they like and do not like about the country.

title: Japan's Top Inventions
subTitle: Discovering the story behind a renowned Japanese product, lotion tissue. Alongside Taniguchi Kenji, who is involved with the development of lotion tissues, reporter Ishii Terumi conducts an experiment on lotion tissues.

title: Japanology Plus
subTitle: Deep dives into cultural phenomena with Peter Barakan, a bilingual British broadcaster based in Japan since 1974. Each time, Peter himself discovers something new about Japan.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Being at War
subTitle: Fighting to ensure their sacrifice wasn't in vain, Ukrainian Special Ops soldier Vlad documents life and death at the front. Discover the raw face of combat through 40 hours of firsthand footage.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Trouble in Paradise
subTitle: Trouble in Paradise

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: The Professionals
subTitle: A documentary series that focuses on first-class professionals from various fields.

title: Dining with the Chef Mini
subTitle: The techniques, ingredients and harmony of Japanese food recipes in just five minutes. Chef Rika cooks alongside master chef Tatsuo Saito to show viewers how to prepare delicious Japanese dishes at home.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Design X Stories
subTitle: Analogue is making a comeback, offering a human appeal to our five senses that digital lacks. Explore designs that reveal the charm and potential of analogue.

title: Design Ah! neo
subTitle: As different colours, blue and yellow can make green by getting together, similarly different lives can coexist by helping each other.

title: Mitateru-Phose: Let's Change Perspective!
subTitle: Using *mitateru*, a Japanese method, to stimulate children's imaginative thinking by prompting them to perceive objects in unconventional ways, fostering their individual viewpoints and inventiveness.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Samurai Castles
subTitle: A look at samurai castles, offering insight into Japanese history and spirituality.

title: Magical Japanese
subTitle: Exploring unique words and the culture behind them.Taking a look at words related to the morning in Japanese. The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature and culture.

title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
subTitle: The Kawanishi Dainenbutsu Kenbai dance tells the story of restless souls being guided to the Pure Land by the Buddha, in the form of a monkey. In 2022, it became UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Spiritual Explorers
subTitle: Sheds light on a range of concepts within Japanese philosophy, spirituality and culture that offer a window into the rich history of a country with its own complex traditions and folklore.

title: National Parks of Japan
subTitle: Daisen-Oki National Park comprises the Oki Islands, which boast spectacular geological formations, and a mainland area with shrines and sacred mountains that gave rise to myths and legends.

title: BOSAI: Science That Can Save Your Life
subTitle: Experts talk about technology that helped people survive the earthquake in Japan and share helpful survival tips.

title: Design Museum Japan
subTitle: Thread for traditional shina fabric takes a year to make, along with ultrafine mohair, made with a 60-year-old device. Nakui admires the hunt for improvement.

title: Inside The Bond Issue Team
subTitle: The filmmaker examines the difficulties encountered by Japan's bond issuance team amid increasing interest rates as the Government of Japan releases a substantial amount of bonds.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Demolition Kingdom
subTitle: We follow the demolition of Japan's World Trade Center Building at 162m. Craftsmen take on the highest demolition in history with world-first new techniques!

title: On the Wings
subTitle: Explores the beautiful sky of Japan from Narita to Kumamoto, including footage from above active volcanoes.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: J-MELO
subTitle: Join `May J' for great Japanese music. This week, `Angela', a duo popular for anime songs, and `SKE48', a Nagoya-based sister group of AKB48, stop by the studio to chat and perform.

title: Little Charo in Tohoku
subTitle: Charo meets up with Budori, a kind raccoon who loves a starry night.

title: Japanology Plus
subTitle: End-of-life planning is spreading in Japan, with many people now thinking hard about what to do with their cremated remains. Exploring diverse graves, including options such as `tree burials.'

title: A Cat's-Eye View of Japan
subTitle: Sanagijima island is home to lots of free-roaming cats. Kuji rests on some cool shrine steps, while other cats hang out close to the sea, carefully making their way along a seawall.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Anime Manga Explosion
subTitle: Discovers the secrets of Japanese anime and manga as their popularity continues to grow. Considers the appeal of these Japanese productions internationally, how they're created, and their cultural significance.

title: The New Adventures of Rockie and Her Friends
subTitle: Rockie accidentally leaks gas and tries to blame someone else. Kapper teaches her a lesson about honesty by putting a spell on her.

title: I Love 'Mee'
subTitle: Everyday questions spark Mee's imagination, leading to wonderful thought experiments that help Mee learn more about Mee's self!

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: The World Heritage
subTitle: Explores important landmarks and cultural heritage sites of the world with spectacular views.

title: Time-Lapse Japan
subTitle: Shimizu Daisuke investigates various aspects of Japanese culture and traditions using the technique of time-lapse photography.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Ramen Japan
subTitle: A journey across Japan exploring the traditions and food culture that surrounds one of its most popular dishes: ramen.

title: Core Kyoto Mini
subTitle: A look at the timeless heart of Japan's ancient capital. Against its rich backdrop of culture and tradition, today's Kyoto continues to innovate and inspire, even after being established 1,200 years ago.

title: Easy Japanese for Work
subTitle: Marianne from Philippines has worked for two years as a housekeeper in Tokyo. Her company wants her service to the next level by learning how to make recommendations.

title: Zero Waste Life
subTitle: Maro Kuratani knits scarves and other items with surplus yarn, using threads of varying colour and thickness. She says yarn is the ultimate eco-friendly material as re-knitting is always an option.

title: Somewhere Street
subTitle: Matera in southern Italy is a town carved into pale limestone cliffs. With its rows of cave dwellings, this city has been home to people for thousands of years and is now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

subTitle: Rockie accidentally leaks gas and tries to blame someone else. Kapper teaches her a lesson about honesty by putting a spell on her.

subTitle: Fascinating experiments that help people visualise sequences and predict future outcomes. Conveys the pleasure found in improving sequence patterns and how programming principles can be discovered in many aspects of daily life.

subTitle: Thread for traditional shina fabric takes a year to make, along with ultrafine mohair, made with a 60-year-old device. Nakui admires the hunt for improvement.

title: Where We Call Home
subTitle: People from around the globe who put down roots in Japan offer a glimpse into their lives and the hardships they face in Japanese society.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Time-Lapse Japan
subTitle: The Jomon period lasted from around 13,000 BC to 300 BC. This society of hunter-gatherers cherished peace, cooperation and connection to the natural world. A look at the Sannai Maruyama site, where 6,000 years ago, a large settlement was established.

title: BOSAI: Science That Can Save Your Life
subTitle: Experts talk about technology that helped people survive the earthquake in Japan and share helpful survival tips.

title: Activate Your Japanese
subTitle: Lessons on how the viewer can make the most out of their Japanese language skills. This time, how to navigate social situations in which everyone has a different opinion, including the viewer.

title: Culture Crossroads
subTitle: Bosai Leaders' promote mutual resident support before and during disasters, and these days foreign participants are playing a significant role. We introduce those being trained in Nakano Ward, Tokyo.

subTitle: Explores various aspects of Hokkaido, ranging from the ecology of brown bears and the craftsmanship of a bespoke bento to a landscape uniquely shaped by the region's rugged natural conditions and pioneering history.

title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
subTitle: In squid-loving Japan, spear squid is immensely popular. Tag along with fishers in Hokkaido and savour some regional cuisine, fresh sushi, and a special take on a beloved Italian-inspired inky dish.

title: NHK World Japan
subTitle: A series of doodles by the famous artist Utagawa Kuniyoshi turn out to be carefully considered depictions of the kabuki actors of the day. We investigate what kind of artistic statement was he trying to make.

title: Medical Frontiers
subTitle: Glaucoma, which can lead to blindness, often shows no symptoms in the early stages. We feature the latest efforts to restore the optic nerve, including new surgical techniques and gene therapy.
